key_insight: EquiFiLM adds a simple charge-conditioning FiLM block to equivariant foundation force fields, enabling accurate modeling of externally driven changes in the potential energy surface without sacrificing E(3)-equivariance.
engineering_relevance: This lets materials simulations capture charging, fields, and excitation-driven processes with near-DFT fidelity using only a small amount of new labeled data and no extra inference cost.
method: Per-layer Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) conditions scalar channels on an external variable such as charge.
Wraps a pretrained equivariant foundation MLFF backbone without changing the core architecture.
Learns charge-dependent energy and force corrections from minimal DFT training data.
evidence_signal: On charged liquid water, E-MACE reduced force RMSE from 21.3 to 6.96 meV/Å and energy RMSE from 6.1 to 0.1 meV/atom on the training charges, while remaining stable across held-out interpolation and extrapolation charges.
watch_item: Performance depends on having representative conditioning data; extrapolation beyond the tested charge range or to different driven physics may still require careful validation.
paper_title: EquiFiLM: Charge-Conditioned Equivariant Force Fields via Feature-wise Linear Modulation
